title: Gnaphalium striatum

intro: Gnaphalium striatum, also known as Striped Cudweed, is a perennial plant species native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, white or pinkish flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties.
